channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Stephen Colbert's next epic quest? Writing a new 'Lord of the Rings' movie
item.description: The film will focus on chapters in J.R.R. Tolkien's The Fellowship of the Ring that were left out of the first movie in the trilogy.
item.content:encoded:
The film will focus on chapters in J.R.R. Tolkien's The Fellowship of the Ring that were left out of the first movie in the trilogy.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: At the Legacy Museum, facing America's racist past is a path, not a punishment
item.description: "There is an America that is more free — where there's more equality, where there is more justice, where there is less bigotry — and I think it's waiting for us," says lawyer Bryan Stevenson.
item.content:encoded: "There is an America that is more free — where there's more equality, where there is more justice, where there is less bigotry — and I think it's waiting for us," says lawyer Bryan Stevenson.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Are you sure you know what 'gaslighting' is?
item.description: Therapists say we're overusing the word. Here's what it actually means — and what the Ingrid Bergman film that helped birth the word can teach us about it.
item.content:encoded:
Therapists say we're overusing the word. Here's what it actually means — and what the Ingrid Bergman film that helped birth the word can teach us about it.

(Image credit: Herbert Dorfman)
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman

item.title: A Pablo Picasso painting is offered for 100 euros in charity raffle
item.description: The painting is worth more than a million euros ($1.2 million). Proceeds from the sale will benefit France's leading financial supporter of Alzheimer's research.
item.content:encoded:
Tête de Femme, which is up for grabs as part of a raffle benefiting an Alzheimer's research charity organized by Péri Cochin, right.'/>

The painting is worth more than a million euros ($1.2 million). Proceeds from the sale will benefit France's leading financial supporter of Alzheimer's research.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Hampton

item.title: How a baseball coach made a teenager feel seen
item.description: Dan Roche, who was born with a cataract that left him mostly blind in one eye, was on a baseball team at age 15. On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, he shares a memory of his coach.
item.content:encoded: Dan Roche, who was born with a cataract that left him mostly blind in one eye, was on a baseball team at age 15. On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, he shares a memory of his coach.

item.title: 'We're all in grief': Chicano artists reckon with Cesar Chavez abuse allegations
item.description: Artists, filmmakers and singers once championed Chavez as a hero. Now, they must rethink how to tell his story. "It's just been gut-wrenching," one muralist says.
item.content:encoded:
Artists, filmmakers and singers once championed Chavez as a hero. Now, they must rethink how to tell his story. "It's just been gut-wrenching," one muralist says.

(Image credit: Lalo Alcaraz)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Her mother murdered her father in an infamous case. Now, she's telling her own story
item.description: Lucille Miller was convicted of killing her husband in 1965. Now her daughter Debra reflects on her own traumatic childhood and its lingering effects in The Most Wonderful Terrible Person.
item.content:encoded: Lucille Miller was convicted of killing her husband in 1965. Now her daughter Debra reflects on her own traumatic childhood and its lingering effects in The Most Wonderful Terrible Person.
item.dc:creator: Ilana Masad

item.title: A professional cornhole player and quadruple amputee is arrested for murder
item.description: Dayton Webber, 27, is accused of shooting a man in his car during an argument. He has shared his story of becoming a pro athlete after losing his arms and legs to a childhood bacterial infection.
item.content:encoded:
Dayton Webber, 27, is accused of shooting a man in his car during an argument. He has shared his story of becoming a pro athlete after losing his arms and legs to a childhood bacterial infection.

(Image credit: Kevin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 8 architecture and culture groups sue Trump and the Kennedy Center board
item.description: The groups, which include the American Institute of Architects, are asking for compliance with historic preservation laws and to secure approval from Congress.
item.content:encoded:
The groups, which include the American Institute of Architects, are asking for compliance with historic preservation laws and to secure approval from Congress.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Riz Ahmed is his own worst critic. His new show 'Bait' explores that
item.description: In his Prime Video series, Ahmed plays a struggling actor auditioning to be the next James Bond. Ahmed says Bond is a "symbol of aspiration, this unattainable kind of self" his character is pursuing.
item.content:encoded:
In his Prime Video series, Ahmed plays a struggling actor auditioning to be the next James Bond. Ahmed says Bond is a "symbol of aspiration, this unattainable kind of self" his character is pursuing.

(Image credit: Tristan Fewings)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley
